back: A lightweight JavaScript representation of the DOM kept in memory, used by React to compute minimal updates before applying changes to the real DOM.
Stale-While-Revalidate — a strategy that shows cached data first, then revalidates in the background and updates the UI when fresh data arrives.
Splitting your bundle into smaller chunks that can be loaded on demand, reducing initial load time and improving performance.

front: What is the difference between SSR and SSG?
back: SSR (Server-Side Rendering) generates HTML on each request. SSG (Static Site Generation) generates HTML at build time and serves the same pre-built page.
When you have an expensive computation that you want to skip on re-renders unless specific dependencies change, to avoid recalculating every time.
